completion_promises.evidence: Added a [project.scripts] block to pyproject.toml (after [project.optional-dependencies]) with `forge = "forge.cli.main:main"`, accompanied by a TASK-PSM-012 comment cross-referencing the entry-point assertion test. Verified by tomllib parse: `data['project']['scripts']['forge'] == 'forge.cli.main:main'`. Test class TestPyprojectScriptsEntry verifies both presence and exact dotted-path value.

Verified with `python3 -m pip install --user --dry-run --no-deps -e . --break-system-packages` — exit 0, no output (success). The build backend (setuptools.build_meta) parses pyproject.toml cleanly with the new [project.scripts] block. Surrogate test test_console_script_target_resolves_to_a_click_group emulates what the pip-installed shim does at invocation time (importlib.import_module('forge.cli.main') and assert isinstance(main, click.Group)) — that operation succeeds.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `forge --help` runs and exits 0; output lists all five subcommands
completion_promises.evidence: Registered the previously-orphaned status_cmd on the main group in src/forge/cli/main.py. CliRunner().invoke(main, ['--help']) returns exit 0 with output listing cancel, history, queue, skip, status. Test test_forge_help_exits_zero_and_mentions_every_subcommand parametrises across all five expected names and asserts every one appears in the rendered help output.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `forge queue --help`, `forge status --help`, `forge history --help`, `forge cancel --help`, `forge skip --help` all exit 0
completion_promises.evidence: TestEverySubcommandHelpExitsZero parametrises over ('queue', 'status', 'history', 'cancel', 'skip') and asserts each `forge <sub> --help` returns exit 0 and renders a Click `Usage:` banner. All five parametrised cases pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Smoke test: `forge status` against an empty SQLite DB returns an empty table (no NATS connection attempted — verified by tracing)
completion_promises.evidence: TestStatusSmokeAgainstEmptyDb fixtures a fresh SQLite database initialised via forge.lifecycle.migrations.apply_at_boot (zero rows). Test test_status_against_empty_db_exits_zero_with_header_only asserts exit 0 and that the BUILD/STATUS column headers render. Test test_status_smoke_does_not_attempt_a_nats_connection patches forge.cli.queue.publish with an AssertionError trip-wire and asserts the status path completes with exit 0 — the trip-wire never fires, proving the status command does not invoke the NATS publisher seam.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `setup.py` / `setup.cfg` are NOT used (this is a `pyproject.toml` project per the existing convention)
completion_promises.evidence: TestPyprojectOnlyBuildSystem verifies that neither setup.py nor setup.cfg exists at the repo root. Both assertions pass — the project remains pyproject.toml-only with setuptools.build_meta as build backend. The TASK-PSM-012 change did not introduce either legacy file.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check src/forge/cli/main.py tests/forge/test_pyproject_console_script.py pyproject.toml -> 'All checks passed!'. black --check --target-version py312 against the same files -> all files pass after applying black formatting to the new test file. No lint or format errors remain on any TASK-PSM-012 touched file.
